name: set_is_noop
code:
proof fn phi_1_set_is_noop(pre_seq: Seq<int>, post_seq: Seq<int>, i: int, out: int, n: int)
    requires
        n > 0,
        0 <= i < n,
        pre_seq.len() == n,
        post_seq =~= pre_seq.update(i, out),
        post_seq.len() == n,
    ensures
        pre_seq =~= post_seq,
{
}
reason: If provable, set never modifies the array, making the operation a no-op regardless of the value written

name: set_corrupts_other_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_set_corrupts_other(pre_seq: Seq<int>, post_seq: Seq<int>, i: int, j: int, out: int, n: int)
    requires
        n > 1,
        0 <= i < n,
        0 <= j < n,
        i != j,
        pre_seq.len() == n,
        post_seq =~= pre_seq.update(i, out),
        post_seq.len() == n,
    ensures
        pre_seq[j] != post_seq[j],
{
}
reason: If provable, set has unintended side effects on indices other than the target index

name: set_ignores_value
code:
proof fn phi_3_set_ignores_value(pre_seq: Seq<int>, post_seq: Seq<int>, i: int, out: int, n: int)
    requires
        n > 0,
        0 <= i < n,
        pre_seq.len() == n,
        post_seq =~= pre_seq.update(i, out),
        post_seq.len() == n,
    ensures
        post_seq[i] != out,
{
}
reason: If provable, set does not actually store the provided value at the target index

name: init2none_first_is_some
code:
proof fn phi_4_init2none_first_is_some(post_seq: Seq<Option<int>>, n: int)
    requires
        n > 0,
        post_seq.len() == n,
        forall|index: int| 0 <= index < n ==> (#[trigger] post_seq[index]).is_None(),
    ensures
        post_seq[0].is_Some(),
{
}
reason: If provable, init2none leaves the first element as Some, directly contradicting its all-None guarantee

name: init2none_empties_seq
code:
proof fn phi_5_init2none_empties_seq(post_seq: Seq<Option<int>>, n: int)
    requires
        n > 0,
        post_seq.len() == n,
        forall|index: int| 0 <= index < n ==> (#[trigger] post_seq[index]).is_None(),
    ensures
        post_seq =~= Seq::<Option<int>>::empty(),
{
}
reason: If provable, init2none collapses the array to an empty sequence instead of preserving length while filling with None
